summary: ErrorLog.kt is a clean, focused 78-LoC implementation that satisfies every Acceptance Criterion of Task 5 and every edge case listed in the task spec. Style, package layout, 2-space indentation, @Singleton + @Inject constructor(@ApplicationContext) pattern, and top-level private const usage all mirror AllowlistLoader.kt exactly. KDoc documents the Phase 1 whitelist (download, inference-init, inference, inference-cleanup), line format, rotation policy, and links to patterns.md § Error logging conventions as required. No duplicate Hilt binding: CoreRuntimeModule.kt contains zero ErrorLog references, so Hilt auto-binding via @Inject constructor is the sole provider. No critical or major findings.

suggestions.suggestion: file.renameTo(rotated) returns a Boolean that is currently ignored. On Android, renameTo can return false (e.g., cross-filesystem or transient FS state) leaving the log oversized; the next e() call will simply attempt rotation again, so behavior is self-healing, but a one-line comment stating this intent would help future readers avoid 'fixing' the ignored return. Optional.
suggestions.benefit: Clarifies that the ignored Boolean is deliberate and that a failed rename is retried on the next write, preventing a reviewer in Task 6+ from adding an erroneous check-and-throw.

suggestions.suggestion: When both cause.message and cause::class.simpleName are null/empty (e.g., anonymous inner Throwable), the sanitized cause becomes an empty string and the final line ends with a trailing ' :: ' with nothing after it. This matches the task's implementation hint verbatim, so not a defect, but a tiny guard — falling back to 'Throwable' when simpleName is empty, or omitting the suffix when the sanitized message is blank — would keep every logged line self-describing. Optional.
suggestions.benefit: Eliminates the rare 'ERROR [component] desc :: ' (trailing empty cause) shape and makes every line end with a non-empty token after '::'.

suggestions.suggestion: runCatching {} with no onFailure/getOrNull follow-up silently discards every I/O exception. This is intentional per the task ('логгер не должен валить вызывающий код') and is the correct contract for a failure-path writer, but there is currently no observability into logger failures at all — if filesDir permissions break in a future Android version the app will appear healthy while every error is dropped. Consider at least a one-shot Logcat println at Log.ASSERT/Log.WTF level on the first catch (guarded by a @Volatile Boolean flag) so platform-level breakage surfaces in bug reports without spamming. Optional and can be deferred to Phase 2+.
suggestions.benefit: Provides a minimal escape hatch for diagnosing catastrophic logger failure without violating the 'no Log.e parallel to file' rule from patterns.md (WTF is reserved for 'this should never happen').
